data.title: Weekly Recap: HSI Climbs As Market Rebounds From Midweek Slump
data.description:
Hong Kong’s stock market recovered from an early-week dip, with the Hang Seng Index (HSI) closing at around 4,122.58 on Jan 23, driven by gains in consumer and select tech-linked stocks.

The week started with the HSI sliding on Jan 19 amid cautious investor sentiment, but buying interest gradually returned midweek, helping the benchmark index regain ground. By Jan 23, key names such as Pop Mart and other consumer-oriented stocks provided momentum, stabilising the broader market.

Market participants noted improved risk appetite as regional equities rebounded alongside easing external pressures, although sentiment remained measured due to lingering macro uncertainties. Smaller growth stocks on the GEM board also saw gains, reflecting renewed confidence among traders.

Analysts say the market will remain sensitive to regional cues and economic data in the coming week, with investors watching for inflows from mainland China and global market trends.

data.title: Weakening Dollar Lifts Chinese Currency
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, strengthened 86 pips to 6.9843 against the U.S. dollar Monday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day. 

The rise of regional currency, including the ringgit, has been attributed to the weakening dollar.

data.title: Market Snapshot: Kospi Nears 5,000 As Stocks Rebound Midweek
data.description:
South Korea’s stock market rebounded midweek, lifting the Kospi index to close at around 4,990.07 on Jan 23, just shy of the symbolic 5,000 mark. Gains were led by technology, automotive and defence stocks, helping the benchmark recover from early-week weakness.

The week began with a modest pullback on Jan 19-20, but buying interest returned midweek, pushing the index higher. Intraday moves briefly nudged the Kospi above 5,000 before trimming late-week gains, reflecting both investor optimism and caution.

Market breadth remained healthy, with blue-chip stocks driving most of the momentum, while foreign and institutional investors continued to influence flows.

Investors remain attentive to both domestic economic data and global market cues as the index tests critical psychological levels in the coming sessions.

data.title: Decliners Outpace Gainers As 561 Counters End In Red
data.description:
The stock market finished weaker on Jan 23 as decliners surpassed gainers amid a cautious trading tone, signalling mild profit-taking ahead of key macro data.

At the close, the market logged 561 losers against 499 gainers, with 578 counters unchanged. Turnover stood at 3.37 billion shares worth RM3.49 billion, reflecting moderating risk appetite late in the session.

Blue-chip laggards exerted pressure on the broader market, with Nestle (M) Bhd fell RM1.80 to RM115.20 topping the loser list, followed by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd dropped 88 sen to RM33.12 and Heineken Malaysia Bhd lost 26 sen to RM22.64. Plantation heavyweights were also weaker, led by United Plantations Bhd who eased 22 sen to RM30.50, while Hong Leong Bank Bhd slipped 20 sen to RM24.

On the upside, selective buying lifted consumer and telecom names. Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd rose 40 sen to RM32.34, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd added 22 sen to RM35.86, while TIME dotCom Bhd climbed 21 sen to RM5.96. Airline operator AirAsia X Bhd gained 18 sen to RM1.84, emerging as one of the most actively traded gainers.

Overall, sentiment remained mixed as investors weighed global market signals and awaited further catalysts, keeping the benchmark and broader indices in consolidation mode.

data.title: South Korean Stocks Finish At Record High, Kospi Nears 5,000 Close
data.description:
South Korea’s stock market ended strongly on Jan 23, with the Kospi closing at an all‑time high of 4,990.07, up 0.76%, as broad buying lifted sentiment and kept the benchmark just shy of the symbolic 5,000 mark.

The index hit an intraday peak above 5,021 before trimming gains, extending a multi‑week rally driven by optimism around tech and financial shares. Institutional and foreign buying underpinned the advance, while the Kosdaq also gained ground, approaching the 1,000‑point milestone on strong biotech and mid‑cap performance.

The strong finish caps an impressive run for Korean equities, which earlier breached the 5,000 level for the first time this week on easing global trade tensions and robust demand for semiconductor exports.

Investors now watch whether the Kospi can sustain levels above 5,000 in the coming sessions amid profit‑taking pressures and rotation among sectors.

data.title: Market Review: Singapore Stocks Finish Strong As STI Logs Weekly Gains
data.description:
Singapore’s equity market closed the week on a positive note, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) climbing and hitting fresh intra-day highs before settling higher on Jan 23. The index ended the week around 4,891.45 points, up about 1.3%, buoyed by broad gains across major bank stocks and optimism in regional markets.

Financial heavyweights drove much of the momentum. UOB and OCBC both surged to record levels, helping lift overall market sentiment, while DBS also contributed modest gains.

Market breadth remained favourable as gainers outnumbered decliners, with the rally tracking broader global stock market strength and positive risk appetite among investors. Despite some underperformance in names like Yangzijiang Shipbuilding, the overall tone stayed upbeat.

Mid-week trading also saw SGX’s own shares edge higher after the exchange proposed cutting standard board lot sizes to improve market accessibility, a development seen as supportive for local equities.

Looking ahead, investors will be watching macro cues and earnings catalysts to sustain momentum as the market enters the new trading week.

data.title: Crypto Weekly Wrap: Bitcoin, Ethereum Hover Near Key Levels Amid Consolidation
data.description:
Bitcoin traded in a relatively tight range this past week, consolidating around the US$87,000-US$90,000 area after rebounding from mid‑week lows. After opening near roughly US$92,000 on Jan 19, Bitcoin pulled back as global macro uncertainty and risk‑off sentiment weighed on crypto markets, then steadied around ~US$89,800 by Jan 23, reflecting sideways price action with compressed volatility.

Ethereum, likewise, experienced choppy trading, with Ethereum dipping from early‑week levels around US$3,200-US$3,340 before stabilising later in the session. Persistent outflows from major Ethereum spot ETFs and cautious positioning among institutional investors contributed to muted performance for the second‑largest crypto, even as modest stabilisation emerged by week’s end.

The week’s price dynamics underscored a broader market in consolidation, with traders awaiting fresh catalysts such as macroeconomic cues or renewed ETF flows to break the current range. Bitcoin’s compressed price bands suggest potential for a significant directional move in the near term, while Ethereum’s trading reflected lingering institutional caution.

Looking ahead, crypto traders will be watching for triggers such as macro data releases, regulatory developments, or shifts in ETF flows to catalyse the next significant move for Bitcoin and Ethereum.

data.title: Singapore Stocks Close Higher As STI Logs Record On Global Rally
data.description:
Singapore shares rallied on Jan 23, with the Straits Times Index (STI) closing 1.3% higher at 4,891.45, just shy of its intraday record peak of 4,895.15 amid broad strength in regional markets and upbeat global sentiment.

Led by robust gains in heavyweight banks, the benchmark index was boosted by financial sector leadership. UOB jumped about 5% to end at S$39.50, while OCBC climbed 3.4% to S$21.29. DBS also contributed with a 1% gain.

Market breadth was positive, with gainers outnumbering losers across the board as investors cheered risk-on cues from global markets. Yangzijiang Shipbuilding lagged as one of the few decliners.

Although Genting Singapore shares slipped modestly, led by modest profit-taking, broader sentiment remained upbeat ahead of key regional policy cues and earnings catalysts.

Overall, the close on Jan 23 capped a strong week for Singapore equities, tracking a global stock market rebound and underpinned by bank sector leadership and solid investor appetite.

data.title: HK Shares Edge Higher On Easing Sentiment
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks closed modestly higher on Jan 23, with the Hang Seng Index rising 0.45% to 26,749.51, underpinned by broad market support as risk sentiment improved across the region.

The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index advanced 0.51% to 9,160.81, while the Hang Seng Tech Index climbed 0.62% to 5,798.01, reflecting selective strength in China-exposed and technology-related names.

Market participants were encouraged by signs of easing geopolitical tensions and supportive macro cues that bolstered Asian equities. This backdrop helped offset lingering concerns about economic headwinds and provided a lift into the weekend session.

Overall, the finish on Jan 23 capped a cautiously optimistic week for Hong Kong equities as traders balanced positive regional sentiment with ongoing macro uncertainties heading into the new year.

data.title: Market Snapshot: KLCI Climbs To 7-Year Peak Despite Mixed Trade
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the week on a firmer note, with the FBM KLCI advancing to 1,719.99 to mark its highest closing level in nearly seven years, even as overall sentiment remained mixed between Jan 19 and Jan 23.

Trading opened cautiously at the start of the week before buyers returned mid-week, lifting key heavyweights and technology names. Mild profit-taking emerged toward the end of the week but failed to derail the benchmark’s upward trajectory.

Broader market indicators were uneven, with the FBM EMAS and other sub-indices seeing alternating gains and pullbacks as turnover stayed healthy. Market breadth reflected a balanced tone, underscoring cautious optimism among investors.

Analysts pointed to supportive domestic factors, including a firmer ringgit and expectations of new market products, as potential drivers for continued interest.

Heading into the coming sessions, market watchers expect the local bourse to adopt a measured stance amid shifting global sentiment and ongoing macro catalysts.

data.title: Ringgit Strengthens To Seven-Year High Against US Dollar Amid BNM Hold
data.description:
The ringgit opened firmer on Monday, hovering near its strongest level in more than seven years following Bank Negara Malaysia’s decision to maintain the overnight policy rate and a weaker US dollar index.

At 8 am, the local currency was quoted at RM3.9850/9950 against the US dollar, up from RM4.0045/0080 recorded last Friday. The ringgit last traded near this level on 24 May 2018, when it touched RM3.9797 against the greenback.

Meanwhile, the ringgit eased against some major currencies. It weakened to 2.5680/5748 versus the Japanese yen, 5.4447/4584 against the British pound, and 4.7258/7377 versus the euro. It also dipped slightly versus the Singapore dollar and Thai baht but inched higher against the Indonesian rupiah and Philippine peso.

The stronger ringgit reflects improved risk appetite among investors following BNM’s decision to maintain its monetary policy stance and signs of softness in the US dollar.

data.title: Silver Vaults Past US$100 Per 28g, Extends Historic Run
data.description:
Silver blasted through the US$100-an-oz mark on Jan 23, cementing its stunning outperformance of gold as a cocktail of retail speculation, ETF inflows and multi-year supply tightness pushed the white metal to fresh highs.

Reuters reported that spot silver jumped 5.1% to US$101, extending a 40% gain in early 2026 after a 147% surge in 2025, its strongest annual rise in LSEG data going back to 1983. The metal’s rally has outpaced even buoyant gold, which notched a record US$4,988 per oz on Jan 23.

Silver’s outperformance has shrunk the gold-silver ratio to 50-to-one for the first time in 14 years, down from 105-to-one last April, a level many traders view as stretched and prone to correction. Technical analysts warn the market’s near-vertical ascent leaves it vulnerable to a sharp pullback.

Behind the surge is a potent mix: Retail buying of bars and coins, inflows into physically backed ETFs and persistent structural deficits in a market where roughly 20% of annual supply comes from recycling. Yet secondary supply has struggled to rebuild amid shortages in high-grade refining capacity, according to Metals Focus.

For now, silver’s blend of precious-safe-haven appeal and industrial demand (from solar to electronics) continues to entice momentum traders. Whether fundamentals or froth are in the driver’s seat may become clearer once liquidity normalises or when the first big correction hits.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum Dip As Risk Aversion Grips Markets
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market is showing signs of pressure, with top-tier tokens Bitcoin and Ethereum posting notable losses on Jan 26 as traders reacted to a cautious macro environment and broader risk sentiment.

Bitcoin prices have weaken sharply, trading around the mid-US$86,000s, down roughly 3.2% over the past 24 hours, as market participants reduced exposure to risk assets and positioned into safer alternatives.

This decline weighed on Bitcoin’s dominant market share and underscored lingering macro uncertainties that have kept traders cautious.

At the same time, Ethereum also extended the downturn, slipping over 5% to the sub-US$2,800 range as bearish sentiment broadened beyond Bitcoin into major altcoins.

The steeper pullback in Ethereum highlights heightened volatility and profit-taking, with traders watching technical support levels closely amid range-bound price action.

On the other hand, the overall market breadth remained weak, with several mid- and large-cap tokens also in the red, even as emerging sectors such as Solana and other layer-1 platforms continued drawing narrative interest.

Analysts attribute the pullback to a broader macro-crypto divergence, where risk assets struggle amid global uncertainty and capital rotation into traditional havens. Investors continue to monitor ETF flows, institutional positioning, and regulatory developments as drivers for renewed momentum.

Moving forward, investors should weigh macro cues and market structure ahead of potential breakouts or deeper corrective phases, while rebounds above critical resistance thresholds may signal stabilisation and a return of risk appetite.

data.title: HSIF Extends Rally, RHB Keeps Long Bias
data.description:
Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) reinforced its bullish setup last Friday after rising 72 points to close at 26,718 points, according to RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research), which reiterated its recommendation to retain long positions.

RHB Research said the index opened at 26,658 points and briefly dipped to an intraday low of 26,656 points before staging a strong rally to a high of 26,951 points.

The session ended with an inverted hammer candlestick pattern, signalling continued buying interest despite some intraday volatility.

During the evening session, HSIF added a further 54 points and was last traded at 26,772 points.

The research house noted that the firm price action above the 20-day simple moving average supports a resilient bullish tone, while the relative strength index moving above the 50% threshold points to improving upside momentum.

These technical signals suggest the index could continue trending higher in the near term, potentially testing the 27,400-point resistance level.

While RHB Research did not rule out short-term profit-taking, it said the broader bullish structure would remain intact as long as HSIF stays above the key 25,000-point support.

The firm maintained a positive trading bias and advised traders to continue holding long positions initiated at 21,416 points, with a stop-loss set at 25,000 points.

Immediate support is seen at 25,600 points, followed by 25,000 points, while resistance levels are identified at 27,400 points and 28,000 points.

data.title: Stock Picks: IHH Healthcare, Pantech, Gas Malaysia And AEON Credit Service
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has highlighted several stocks showing bullish technical signals following recent breakouts above key resistance levels.

The research house said IHH Healthcare Bhd is extending its upside momentum after staging a bullish breakout, with the stock closing above the RM8.55 resistance.

RHB Research noted that the positive momentum could see the counter move towards RM8.95 and subsequently RM9.30.

However, the bullish setup would be negated if the share price falls below the RM8.20 support level, which could trigger a correction phase.

Pantech Group Bhd was also flagged after breaking above the RM0.69 resistance on strong volume.

RHB Research said the bullish candlestick formation indicates strong positive momentum, with the stock potentially testing RM0.73 before moving towards RM0.77.

A drop below the RM0.66 support would invalidate the bullish outlook.

Meanwhile, Gas Malaysia is seen approaching a potential continuation breakout after completing a consolidation phase.

RHB Research observed renewed buying interest as the stock moves closer to the RM4.55 resistance.

A decisive break above this level could lift the counter towards RM4.70, with a higher resistance at RM4.85.

The bullish setup would be nullified if prices slip below RM4.40.

Aeon Credit Service (M) was also cited as being in an upward trend after closing above the RM6.05 resistance.

RHB Research said the stock has remained above its 21-day simple moving average, signalling that the short-term trend remains bullish.

Following the breakout, the counter could advance towards RM6.40 and RM6.65, while a fall below RM5.80 would invalidate the current setup.

data.title: Bursa Kicks Off Week On A Positive Note, FBM KLCI Edges Up 0.17%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Monday, with the benchmark FBM KLCI rising 2.86 points or 0.17% to 1,722.85 at 9.06 am, led by gains in key blue-chip and mid-cap counters.

The broader market also saw positive movements, with the FBM70 up 38.82 points (+0.22%) to 17,601.04, the F4GBM rising 1.81 points (+0.18%) to 1,020.82 and the FBMEMAS gaining 24.78 points (+0.20%) to 12,685.32. FBMSHA rising 7.80 points (+0.06%) to 12,317.45.

Among the most active stocks, CapitaLand Malaysia Investment (CAPITALA) climbed 1.5 sen to 56 sen on 9.46 million shares, while Versatile Solutions (VS) slipped half a sen to 45.5 sen on 4.99 million shares. AAX Group rose 8 sen to RM1.92 with 4 million shares traded.

Top gainers included UTDPLT (+RM0.32 to RM30.82), Nestle Malaysia (+RM0.30 to RM115.50), HEIM (+RM0.16 to RM22.80), HL Bank (+RM0.14 to RM24.14) and PETGAS (+RM0.10 to RM18.68).

On the downside, IHH Healthcare fell 10 sen to RM8.50, TM lost 8 sen to RM7.88, UWC dropped 8 sen to RM4.17, Malayan Cement eased 7 sen to RM7.76 and Vitrox slipped 6 sen to RM4.49.

Market participants said early gains reflected cautious optimism among investors, although trading remained selective, with liquidity concentrated in mid- and small-cap stocks.

data.title: Wall Street Ends Mixed As Intel Slump Caps Volatile Week
data.description:
US stocks closed out a whipsaw week on a cautious note on Jan 23, as a sharp drop in Intel soured investor sentiment and nudged the Dow lower, Reuters reported.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 285.30 points, or 0.58%, to 49,098.71, while the S&P 500 eked out a 0.03% gain to 6,915.61. The Nasdaq Composite rose 0.28% to 23,501.24, lifted by strength in megacap tech.

The subdued finish followed a two-day rebound from the tariff-driven rout on Jan 20, when US President Donald Trump rattled markets with threats to impose levies on European allies in a bizarre bid to advance his claim on Greenland. Still, all three major benchmarks posted weekly losses: The S&P 500 slipped 0.36%, the Dow fell 0.53% and the Nasdaq eased 0.06%.

Sentiment took a fresh hit on Jan 23 after Intel tumbled 17% on a gloomy revenue and profit outlook, with the chipmaker conceding it had struggled to meet demand for data centre server chips powering artificial intelligence (AI) workloads.

With tech and semiconductor names still trading at hefty valuations, many on Wall Street see 2026 as a “show-me” year, where massive AI-driven capex must translate into tangible earnings growth.

Investors won’t have to wait long for answers as earnings from several Magnificent Seven heavyweights, including Apple, Tesla and Microsoft, land next week.

Despite the Intel drag, megacaps mostly climbed, with Microsoft, Meta and Amazon up between 1.7% and 3.3%. Nvidia gained 1.5% following a report that Chinese regulators have allowed tech giants including Alibaba, Tencent and ByteDance to prepare orders for its H200 AI chips.

Seven of the S&P’s 11 major sub-sectors finished higher, led by materials, up 0.9%. Energy rose 0.6% to notch a third straight record close, cementing its position as the week’s best performer. The sector is also the standout of 2026 so far, up 10.1% year-to-date.

Despite geopolitical jitters and sector-specific setbacks, investors generally remain confident in the resilience of the US economy, even if volatility is back in the frame.

Volume was firm, with 17.34 billion shares traded across US exchanges, above the 20-day average of 17.07 billion.

data.title: SGX Opens Lower Amid MAS Policy Outlook, Semiconductor-Led Growth
data.description:
Singapore’s stock market opened lower on Monday as investors weighed robust economic growth against the prospect of the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) maintaining current monetary policy settings later this week.

At 9.06 am, the Straits Times Index (STI) stood at 4,879.25 points, down 12.20 points or 0.25% from Friday’s close, with 112 stocks advancing and 76 declining on a total volume of 153.81 million shares worth S$171.96 million.

Bank counters led the early weakness, with UOB down 2.66% to S$38.45. DBS, OCBC, CapitaLand Investment, Singtel, ST Engineering and YZJ Shipbuilding also traded lower in early deals.

Derivatives trading showed 29,340 contracts exchanged, with SGX FTSE Taiwan Index Futures at 2,586.75 and Nikkei 225 Futures at 53,065.00.

Market watchers said investor sentiment was influenced by expectations that MAS will keep monetary policy unchanged at its review on Thursday.

Singapore’s GDP expanded 4.8% in 2025, exceeding the government’s November forecast of around 4.0%, supported by strong semiconductor demand and a steady tech cycle, with the electronics PMI at 50.9 in December.

Economist Tay Qi Hang from the Economist Intelligence Unit Asia noted that AI-related demand and rising memory chip prices are likely to continue supporting the sector.

Standard Chartered chief economist Edward Lee said inflation remains under control, reducing pressure for immediate action, though MAS may tighten policy in April.

Bank of America economists flagged the possibility of an earlier tightening following December inflation data, which showed price increases in travel-related and other sectors offsetting declines in raw food and beverage prices.

MAS manages monetary conditions by letting the Singapore dollar trade within a band against currencies of its main trading partners, adjusting the slope, midpoint and width of the band as needed.

Globally, major central banks are expected to hold rates steady in the near term, although market uncertainty remains over the US Federal Reserve’s independence.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Dips At Midday As Heavyweights See Mild Profit-Taking
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its morning losses at midday as investors engaged in profit-taking on selected heavyweight stocks.

By 12:30pm, the FBM KLCI had slipped 1.36 points, or 0.08%, to 1,715.78 from yesterday’s close of 1,717.14. Among major indices, the FBM 70 rose 16.26 points to 17,599.00, while the FBM EMAS slipped 3.96 points to 12,645.39. The FBM SHA posted a modest gain of 7.95 points to 12,323.20. The F4GBM, meanwhile, also declined 0.66 points to 1,017.77.

The overall market breadth, meanwhile, turned negative with 476 decliners versus 453 gainers. Some 548 counters remained unchanged, 1,253 were untraded and 21 suspended. Total turnover hit 1.77 billion shares worth RM1.47 billion.

High-volume movers from the morning trading session were Zetrix AI Bhd at 81.5 sen with trading volume of 64.6 million shares, followed by Capital A Bhd at 54.5 sen after gaining 2.5 sen with 54.1 million shares changing hand and Tanco Holdings Bhd losing two sen to trade at RM1.29 with more than 40 million shares traded. Other active stocks include Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd at RM7.51 after marginal increase of one sen with 35.4 million shares and Velesto Energy Bhd at 30.5 sen with 33.8 million shares.

Nestle (M) Bhd continues to lead the decliners as the counter dropped RM2 to RM115, followed by United Plantations Bhd easing 30 sen to RM30.42 and Hong Leong Bank Bhd falling 22 sen to RM23.98. On the flipside, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd (F&N), Hong Leong Industries Bhd and Perusahaan Sadur Timah Malaysia (Perstima) Bhd attracted buying interest, reflecting steady demand for selective consumer and industrial stocks. F&N and Hong Leong Industries added 20 sen to close their midday trading at RM35.84 and RM17.18, respectively, while Perstima gained 18 sen to RM1.98.

Overall, the mixed performance highlights a cautious market tone, with investors prioritising liquidity and trimming positions in high-profile stocks while monitoring activity in mid- and small-cap counters.

Analysts say the pullback reflects routine profit-taking rather than a broad sentiment shift, with investors keeping a close eye on heavyweight and high-volume counters for signals in the afternoon session.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Jan 23, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on Jan 23, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.
